source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/09_Usability/250504 파생모델 추가/2. A-UVR-HNX_Usability Validation Report (Rev.1).docx

document.title: Usability Validation Report for HnX-P1, HnX-PB Portable X-ray System
standards: FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Validation Report Rev.1 for HnX-P1 and variant model HnX-PB Portable X-ray System.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents usability validation activities, applicable standards and guidance, test scenarios, results, and conclusion for the Portable X-ray System.
keyPoints: Rev.1 adds the variant model HnX-PB.
HnX-PB is stated to have only exterior design changes compared with HnX-P1, with interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software the same.
No additional usability validation will be conducted for the addition of HnX-PB.
The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Testing included 15 radiology technician participants and reported 100% task completion success rate with Pass result.
The conclusion states residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

entities.name: HnX-P1
entities.evidence: Portable X-ray System, Model HnX-P1 & HnX-PB

entities.name: FDA
entities.evidence: FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices

concepts.name: Usability Validation
concepts.evidence: Validation activities were conducted to evaluate the usability of the HnX-P1 in simulated real-world environments.

concepts.name: Human Factors and Usability Engineering
concepts.evidence: FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices

concepts.name: Summative Evaluation
concepts.evidence: The summative evaluation is an integral part of the validation process, providing final confirmation that the device meets usability requirements

concepts.name: Use Error
concepts.evidence: The re-evaluation of usability will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.

concepts.name: Residual Usability Risk
concepts.evidence: residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable

concepts.name: Variant Model
concepts.evidence: The variant model(HnX-PB) has only changed the exterior design compared to the existing model HnX-P1

evidence.claim: The document is a Usability Validation Report for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB Portable X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Report for HnX-P1, HnX-PB Portable X-ray System

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-UVR-HNX Rev.1.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-UVR-HNX (Rev.1)

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on May 04, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Jeong, Yeji May 04, 2025; Review R&D team Han, jimin May 04, 2025; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu May 04, 2025

evidence.claim: Revision 1 dated May 04, 2025 added the variant model HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: 1 May 04, 2025 Add variant model (HnX-PB)

evidence.claim: HnX-PB is described as differing from HnX-P1 only in exterior design, with interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model(HnX-PB) has only changed the exterior design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: No additional usability validation will be conducted for adding HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, no additional usability validation will be conducted for the addition of the variant model(HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The device is intended to obtain diagnostic information using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR, or DR.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).

evidence.claim: The Portable X-ray System is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The Portable X-ray System is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Portable X-ray System Indications for Use

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Applicable standards and guidance listed include EN 60601-1-6:2010, IEC 62366-1:2015, FDA human factors guidance, and ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010; IEC 62366-1:2015; FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices; ISO 14971

evidence.claim: Validation activities evaluated usability of HnX-P1 in simulated real-world environments.
evidence.evidenceText: Validation activities were conducted to evaluate the usability of the HnX-P1 in simulated real-world environments.

evidence.claim: The validation objectives were to verify safe and effective use by intended users and identify and mitigate residual usability risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Verify that the product can be used safely and effectively by intended users. Identify and mitigate any residual usability risks.

evidence.claim: The user groups were radiologists and clinicians.
evidence.evidenceText: User Groups: Radiologists and clinicians.

evidence.claim: Use environments were hospital settings and mobile clinics.
evidence.evidenceText: Use Environments: Hospital settings and mobile clinics.

evidence.claim: The test included 15 participants described as radiology technicians.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of Participants: 15 Demographics: Radiology technicians

evidence.claim: Test locations included Hope Hospital (Byeongjeom) and Samyuk Seoul Hospital.
evidence.evidenceText: Location 장소 Hope Hospital (Byeongjeom) 희망찬병원(병점) Samyuk Seoul Hospital 삼육서울병원

evidence.claim: Task completion success criterion was 100% completion in each usability task, and the result was 100% Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. Result: 100 % (Pass)

evidence.claim: Eleven usability activities were reported as Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: ACT #1 ACT #2 ACT #3 ACT #4 ACT #5 ACT #6 ACT #7 ACT #8 ACT #9 ACT #10 ACT #11 Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass

evidence.claim: The conclusion states usability residual risk is minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

evidence.claim: The report states no benefit-outweigh-risk evaluation was performed because the usability goal met acceptance criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Since the usability verification & evaluation report demonstrates that the usability goal met the acceptance criteria set in the usability verification & evaluation plan, no evaluation of “benefit outweigh risk” is performed.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is identified only through a cited FDA guidance; the text does not state an FDA submission, clearance, approval, or jurisdictional determination.
Scope text refers to Model HnX-P1, while title, purpose, revision history, and variant-model statements include HnX-PB.
Some acceptance criteria are phrased as 'over 100%' or 'more than 100%', which appears unusual but results are consistently reported as 100%.
Source text contains mixed Korean and English table extraction artifacts, but key metadata and conclusions remain readable.
